summary: The diff implements most of the requested features. REQ-1 (backend proxyUrl) and REQ-2 (frontend useProxyUrl mutation) are substantially implemented. REQ-3 (wiring into BrowserLayout.tsx) is mentioned in the file summary but BrowserLayout.tsx content is not shown in the diff. The transform callback exists but proxyUrl returns only Text, not a structured type with headers/status. Several acceptance criteria gaps exist.

missing_requirements.requirement: The function returns the response body AND HTTP status code (and relevant headers like content-type).
missing_requirements.reason: The proxyUrl function in backend/main.mo returns only async Text (the response body). There is no structured return type carrying HTTP status code or content-type headers.

missing_requirements.requirement: On error, it surfaces an error message usable by the UI.
missing_requirements.reason: The useProxyRequest mutation in useQueries.ts has no explicit error handling or error message shaping — it simply re-throws the raw actor error with no user-facing transformation.

missing_requirements.requirement: Wire the direct URL proxy mutation into BrowserLayout.tsx so that entering a URL triggers proxyUrl, proxied HTML is passed to ProxyContent, loading/error states are reflected, and address bar URL is updated after success.
missing_requirements.reason: BrowserLayout.tsx is listed only in the file summary as modified but its actual diff content is not shown. Cannot verify from available evidence that all REQ-3 acceptance criteria are met.

hallucinated_features.description: Migration module (backend/migration.mo) that drops stable variables from a previous actor version.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request makes no mention of a migration module or dropping stable variables. This is an entirely new file not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: useSearchRequest now uses DuckDuckGo HTML search via actor.proxyUrl instead of Google Custom Search.
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request explicitly lists modifying the Google Custom Search integration as a non-goal. The diff replaces the search backend with DuckDuckGo parsing, violating this constraint.
